text:
combine in their
transits. This is the opinion of Prthuyasas. If the Sun is in
conjunction with the Moon, the subject will become a mechanic
or mason; if with Mars, intent on committing sins; if with
Mercury, clever and possessed of intelligence, fame and happi-
ness; if with Jupiter, cruel and ready to help others; if with Venus,
he earns wealth by means of weapons or acting; and if with
Saturn, becomes clever in metallurgy or pottery.
Moon :—If the Moon is with Mars, the subject deals in ham-
mers, ploughs, women, liquor and pots, and will be bad for his
mother; if with Mercury, he speaks sweetly and modestly, be-
comes clever in economics, and possessed of happiness and
fame; with Jupiter, becomes heroic, leader of family, of unsteady
mind and very rich; if with Venus, becomes clever in selling and
buying cloths; and if with Saturn, he becomes the son of a
woman remarried. Though the combination of the Moon and
Mars is extolled by Parasara as an excellent Yoga (Candra-
